LibraryMember.title: LibraryMember
LibraryMember.description: Represents a user registered with the NEU Library, holding information pertinent to their library activities (e.g., loans, reservations). Authentication details are managed externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LibraryMember entity.

externalAuthId.description: Identifier from the external authentication system, linking the library profile to an authenticated user.

firstName.description: The first name of the library member.

lastName.description: The last name of the library member.

email.description: The email address of the library member.

studentId.description: The student ID of the library member.

membershipDate.description: The date and time when the member joined the library.

borrowedBookIds.description: References to Books currently borrowed by this member. (Relationship: LibraryMember N:N Book via Loan)

reservedBookIds.description: References to Books currently reserved by this member. (Relationship: LibraryMember N:N Book via Reservation)

Book.title: Book
Book.description: Represents a single book available in the NEU Library's catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Book entity.

isbn.description: The International Standard Book Number (ISBN) of the book.

publicationYear.description: The year the book was published.

genreIds.description: References to Genres associated with this book. (Relationship: Genre N:N Book)

description.description: A brief summary or abstract of the book.

totalCopies.description: The total number of physical copies of this book owned by the library.

availableCopies.description: The number of copies currently available for borrowing.

coverImageUrl.description: URL to the book's cover image.

Author.title: Author
Author.description: Represents an author of one or more books in the library.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Author entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the author.

lastName.description: The last name of the author.

biography.description: A short biographical sketch of the author.

Publisher.title: Publisher
Publisher.description: Represents a publisher of books in the library's catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Publisher entity.

name.description: The name of the publishing company.

headquartersLocation.description: The primary location of the publisher's headquarters.

Genre.title: Genre
Genre.description: Represents a category or genre under which books are classified.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Genre entity.

name.description: The name of the genre (e.g., 'Science Fiction', 'History').

Loan.title: Loan
Loan.description: Represents an instance of a specific book copy being borrowed by a library member.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Loan entity.

loanDate.description: The date and time when the book was borrowed.

dueDate.description: The date and time when the book is due for return.

returnDate.description: The date and time when the book was actually returned. Null if not yet returned.

status.description: The current status of the loan (e.g., 'borrowed', 'overdue', 'returned').

Reservation.title: Reservation
Reservation.description: Represents a request by a library member to reserve a book that is currently unavailable.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Reservation entity.

reservationDate.description: The date and time when the reservation was placed.

pickupReadyDate.description: The date and time when the book became available for pickup. Null if not yet ready.

expiryDate.description: The date and time by which the reserved book must be picked up.

status.description: The current status of the reservation (e.g., 'pending', 'ready_for_pickup', 'cancelled', 'fulfilled').

definition.description: Stores individual library member profiles. The document ID (`libraryMemberId`) MUST be the Firebase Auth UID of the member. The `externalAuthId` field within the document also stores this UID for explicit authorization independence. Accessible only by the owning user or administrators.

params.name: libraryMemberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the library member, which corresponds to the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

definition.description: Catalog of all books available in the library. This collection is publicly readable by all users (authenticated or not).

params.name: bookId
params.description: The unique identifier of the book.

definition.description: Catalog of authors. This collection is publicly readable by all users (authenticated or not).

params.name: authorId
params.description: The unique identifier of the author.

definition.description: Catalog of publishers. This collection is publicly readable by all users (authenticated or not).

params.name: publisherId
params.description: The unique identifier of the publisher.

definition.description: Catalog of genres. This collection is publicly readable by all users (authenticated or not).

params.name: genreId
params.description: The unique identifier of the genre.

definition.description: Stores loan records specific to a library member. The `memberId` field within the document is denormalized to match `libraryMemberId` from the path and `request.auth.uid` for explicit authorization independence. Accessible only by the owning user or administrators.

params.name: libraryMemberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the library member, which corresponds to the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

params.name: loanId
params.description: The unique identifier of the loan.

definition.description: Stores reservation records specific to a library member. The `memberId` field within the document is denormalized to match `libraryMemberId` from the path and `request.auth.uid` for explicit authorization independence. Accessible only by the owning user or administrators.

params.name: libraryMemberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the library member, which corresponds to the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

params.name: reservationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the reservation.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for storing library administrator roles. Document IDs are the Firebase UIDs of users designated as administrators. Used for database-backed access control (DBAC) to grant elevated privileges to library staff for managing all data.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of a user who holds the library administrator role.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for the NEU Library App prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization using the outlined core design principles and mandates. It employs a hybrid approach, segregating public catalog data from private user-specific data, and utilizing a database-backed access control (DBAC) model for administrative roles.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

1.  **LibraryMember Profile:** The `/libraryMembers/{libraryMemberId}` collection uses the authenticated user's Firebase UID as the `libraryMemberId` (document ID). This inherently ties the document to the user. Additionally, the `externalAuthId` field within the `LibraryMember` document itself will store this same UID. This denormalization ensures that authorization rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the document ID and/or the `externalAuthId` field within the document, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to other documents to determine ownership.
2.  **Loans and Reservations:** For user-specific data like loans and reservations, a hierarchical structure `/libraryMembers/{libraryMemberId}/loans/{loanId}` and `/libraryMembers/{libraryMemberId}/reservations/{reservationId}` is used. Critically, each `Loan` and `Reservation` document will contain a `memberId` field. This `memberId` field *must* match the `libraryMemberId` in the path and `request.auth.uid`. This explicit denormalization within each document ensures that rules can check the `memberId` field directly without needing to `get()` the parent `LibraryMember` document, making operations atomic and simplifying security rules significantly.

**Support for Query-Aware Permissions (QAPs):**

1.  **Public Catalog Data:** Collections such as `/books`, `/authors`, `/publishers`, and `/genres` are designed to be publicly readable. This uniform security posture allows for secure `list` operations without requiring complex filtering logic in rules. Any user, authenticated or not, can query and retrieve a list of these entities, satisfying the QAP principle.
2.  **Private User Data:** For user-specific `Loan`s and `Reservation`s, the path structure `/libraryMembers/{libraryMemberId}/loans/{loanId}` allows a logged-in user to query *only their own* loans or reservations by making a `list` query against `/libraryMembers/$(request.auth.uid)/loans`. The rules will enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches `libraryMemberId` in the path segment, ensuring that users can only retrieve their private data without inadvertently exposing other users' information. The denormalized `memberId` within each document further reinforces this, making these `list` operations inherently secure and efficient.

**Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC):**

To manage administrative access (e.g., library staff who can manage all loans/reservations), a dedicated collection `/roles_libraryAdmins/{adminUid}` is introduced. This collection stores documents where the document ID is the Firebase UID of an administrator. Security rules can then simply check for the existence of a document at `/roles_libraryAdmins/$(request.auth.uid)` to grant elevated privileges, adhering to the DBAC principle and avoiding custom claims.

This structure provides clear separation of data based on its security posture, enables robust authorization independence, and supports secure `list` operations for both public and private data, leading to a highly debuggable and maintainable security ruleset.
